verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN48) is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein predominantly resident in the trans-Golgi network, but it constitutively cycles between the TGN and the plasma membrane via secretory vesicles. A documented minority surface pool exists at steady state, with continuous retrieval back to the TGN via endosomes. Its ectodomain has been detected at the cell surface and is also shed. This is a textbook dual-localization / TGN-PM cycling protein — the TM precursor transits the PM with its ectodomain exposed extracellularly during the cycle.
